properties.description: Merges, cleans, and deduplicates CRM CSV and Excel customer records into a single unified output stream.

sources.name: CRMSource
sources.description: CRM CSV weekly export — snake_case columns

sources.name: ExcelSource
sources.description: Sales team Excel workbook — PascalCase columns

sinks.name: SinkCSV
sinks.description: Write clean output to data/clean/ in Azure Blob Storage.

sinks.name: SinkSQL
sinks.description: Load clean rows into dbo.CustomerStaging in SQL Server.

transformations.name: SelectCRM
transformations.description: Rename CRM snake_case columns to unified PascalCase schema and add SourceSystem = 'CRM'.

transformations.name: SelectExcel
transformations.description: Rename Excel PascalCase columns to unified schema and add SourceSystem = 'Excel'.

transformations.name: DerivedCRM
transformations.description: Apply business rules to CRM stream: cast types, normalise formats, add SourceSystem.

columns.name: Country
columns.expression: case(Country == 'Egypt', 'Egypt', Country == 'Saudi Arabia', 'Saudi Arabia', Country == 'UAE', 'United Arab Emirates', Country == 'Jordan', 'Jordan', Country == 'Kuwait', 'Kuwait', Country)

transformations.name: DerivedExcel
transformations.description: Apply business rules to Excel stream: cast types, map ISO country codes, normalise phone with country code prefix, add SourceSystem.

columns.name: SignupDate
columns.expression: case(!isNull(SignupDate) && length(trim(SignupDate)) > 0, case(toInteger(SignupDate) > 40000, toDate(addDays(toDate('1899-12-30', 'yyyy-MM-dd'), toInteger(SignupDate))), toDate(SignupDate, 'dd/MM/yyyy')), null())

columns.name: Country
columns.expression: case(Country == 'EGY', 'Egypt', Country == 'SAU', 'Saudi Arabia', Country == 'ARE', 'United Arab Emirates', Country == 'JOR', 'Jordan', Country == 'KWT', 'Kuwait', Country == 'USA', 'United States', Country == 'GBR', 'United Kingdom', Country)

transformations.name: FilterCRM
transformations.description: Drop CRM rows where CustomerID is null/zero or CustomerName is null/blank.

transformations.name: FilterExcel
transformations.description: Drop Excel rows where CustomerID is null/zero or CustomerName is null/blank.

transformations.name: UnionStreams
transformations.description: Append both cleaned streams into one combined dataset.

transformations.name: DeduplicateByCustomerID
transformations.description: Keep exactly one record per CustomerID. CRM is preferred over Excel. When both sources have the same CustomerID the CRM values are used for all key fields.
